hardcoded_triggers.response: IIT Mandi offers 11 distinct B.Tech disciplines, 1 Bachelor of Science (BS) program, and 1 Integrated Master's program under JoSAA: 1. Computer Science & Engineering (CSE), 2. Data Science & Artificial Intelligence (DS-AI), 3. Mathematics & Computing (MnC), 4. Electrical Engineering (EE), 5. Microelectronics & VLSI, 6. Mechanical Engineering (ME), 7. Civil Engineering (CE), 8. Engineering Physics (EP), 9. Bio-Engineering, 10. Materials Science & Engineering, 11. General Engineering, 12. BS in Chemical Sciences, and 13. Integrated MBA (IMBA).

The total undergraduate intake capacity at IIT Mandi is roughly 520 seats across all branches (including female supernumerary quotas). The approximate seat distribution per branch includes: CSE (80), EE (70), ME (60), CE (50), DS-AI (50), EP (30), Bio-Engg (30), General Engg (30), Materials Science (30), Microelectronics & VLSI (30), MnC (30), and BS Chemical Sciences (30). Make sure to check the live JoSAA seat matrix dashboard for the absolute final seat counts.

keywords: can i change branch to cse
hardcoded_triggers.response: IIT Mandi features a highly flexible 'Double Major' system introduced from 2021. This allows a student in any core branch (like Civil or Mechanical) to earn a second major degree in another field (including Computer Science & Engineering) by completing approximately 12 additional specialized courses across an extra 1 to 2 semesters. Minor specializations (3 to 4 extra courses) are also available in areas like Management, Intelligent Systems, and German language.

For candidates looking at integrated profiles, IIT Mandi the School of Management offers a 5-year Integrated Master of Business Administration (IMBA) program directly open to students after their Class 12 board qualifications through JEE Advanced allocations.

The tuition fee for the 4-year B.Tech/BS program at IIT Mandi is approximately ₹1,00,000 per semester for General, EWS, and OBC candidates (total academic expenses hover around ₹8.25 Lakhs across 4 years). Candidates should note that 100% tuition fee waivers are applicable for SC, ST, and PwD candidates, and partial-to-full waivers exist for economically weaker sections based on verified family income declarations.

IIT Mandi follows a strict semester system. The academic year is divided into two terms: the Odd Semester (running from early August through late November/early December) and the Even Semester (running from February through June). Each semester runs roughly 18 weeks, which includes instructional days, mid-semester checks, and end-semester evaluations.
